title: Twin blasts in Punjab: Explosion near Amritsar Cantt, scooter blast near Jalandhar BSF HQ
content: A blast near Amritsar's Khasa cantonment and a scooter fire in Jalandhar have sparked political outcry against the Punjab government. While no casualties were reported in Amritsar, opposition parties criticized the Aam Aadmi Party for alleged governance failures and deteriorating law and order, especially given Punjab's sensitive border status.

title: IPL playoff scenarios: Delhi Capitals staring at elimination after another home loss
content: Delhi Capitals' IPL 2026 playoff hopes are fading fast after a string of losses, exposing critical issues in their batting, tactical confusion, and pressure handling. With only four games left and a negative net run rate, they face a daunting task to reach the required points. Their upcoming fixtures against strong opponents further complicate their precarious position.

title: Don't listen to CEOs: Meta's most-famous ex-employee AI Yann to students
content: AI pioneer Yann LeCun urges students to disregard alarmist CEO pronouncements and fears of AI-driven job losses and extinction. He emphasizes that higher education, particularly in fields like physics and electrical engineering, will become more valuable as AI tools require human oversight and strategic direction, leading to job evolution rather than elimination.

title: IPL 2026: No fortress, no momentum — Delhi Capitals’ season unravels at home
content: Chennai Super Kings dominated the Delhi Capitals on and off the field in IPL 2026 on Tuesday. Delhi’s batting faltered again, despite late resistance from Tristan Stubbs and Sameer Rizvi. Poor home form and inconsistent pitches have left DC’s playoff hopes slim.

title: As Dell CEO to move company’s base to Texas; gets message from governor Greg Abbott
content: Dell Technologies is moving its legal home from Delaware to Texas. This change is set for approval at the company's annual stockholders meeting on June 25, 2026. Texas Governor Greg Abbott welcomed the tech giant, highlighting its long history in the state.

title: IPL: Rahul overtakes Abhishek to reclaim Orange Cap; Samson jumps to fifth
content: DC opener KL Rahul has regained the IPL 2026 Orange Cap after scoring 12 runs against CSK. He now leads with 445 runs, narrowly ahead of Abhishek Sharma. Sanju Samson's unbeaten 87 propelled him to fifth place. The top five batsmen are separated by just 43 runs, promising an exciting race.

title: UK-based Google DeepMind employees voted to form world’s first workers union
content: Google DeepMind UK employees are forming the world's first union at a frontier AI lab to protest the company's AI deal with the US government. Workers aim to halt the use of Google's AI tools by the US Department of War and the Israeli military, seeking to reinstate ethical commitments and gain a voice in company decisions.

title: IPL playoff qualification scenarios: Revived Chennai Super Kings back in control
content: Chennai Super Kings reignited their IPL 2026 playoff hopes with a dominant win over Delhi Capitals. Sanju Samson's unbeaten 87 steered them to a comfortable chase after disciplined bowling. Now sixth, CSK need at least two wins from their remaining four games, including crucial ties against Lucknow Super Giants, to secure a top-four spot amidst a tight race.

title: Taylor Swift amps up security for July wedding to Travis Kelce amid safety risks
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are set to step into one of the most exciting phases of their lives as the world waits for their multi million dollar wedding which is slated to be one of the biggest and exciting events of the year. While there are speculations that the high profile couple have already locked in on 3rd July as their wedding date, it seems Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce might just be planning something else too.

title: Florida mom arrested after rant, accused of kicking child at youth game
content: Renee Lambert was arrested in Fort Myers after she allegedly kicked a child during a youth football game at Brooks Park. Police say she entered a player fight and later argued with officers during arrest. She faces child abuse and resisting charges. Authorities found no proof of her claim that a child hit her first. Court date is June 1.

title: Forest Star Shares Graphic Head Injury After Chelsea Win
content: Nottingham Forest’s 3–1 win over Chelsea was overshadowed by a head collision between Gibbs-White and goalkeeper Robert Sánchez, both requiring stitches. Gibbs-White had assisted the third goal before the clash, while Sánchez suffered a smaller wound. Despite the injuries, both appeared stable, with social media posts in good spirits. Forest remain six points clear of relegation, focusing now on their Europa League semi-final.

title: 'Project Freedom will be paused for a short period': Trump halts Hormuz op amid Iran talks
content: President Trump has halted 'Project Freedom,' a US military initiative to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz, citing requests from Pakistan and progress toward a deal with Iran. This pause comes after the US declared the offensive phase of 'Operation Epic Fury' complete, emphasizing defensive actions in the region.

title: Mamata 'Bye'-nerjee: BJP tide leaves Bengal's big fish out of water
content: Bengal has witnessed a seismic political shift as the BJP surged to power, unseating Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress after 15 years. The saffron party secured a decisive victory, breaching the majority mark and fulfilling Amit Shah's ambitious target. This marks a significant change in the state's political landscape, ending decades of Left and TMC dominance.

title: Mystery explosion outside BSF Punjab Frontier headquarters
content: In a startling incident, a powerful explosion erupted near the BSF Punjab Frontiers headquarters in Jalandhar on Tuesday evening, targeting a courier's scooter. The injured, identified as Gurpreet Singh, has expressed concern that the blast was the result of a bomb thrown at him.

title: Slow and unsteady: Has Jasprit Bumrah’s once-lethal variation lost its edge?
content: Jasprit Bumrah’s trademark slower ball has struggled in IPL 2026, conceding 74 runs from 46 deliveries without taking a wicket. Bumrah has managed only three wickets in 10 matches, with critics like Sunil Gavaskar pointing to declining pace, inconsistent lengths and overuse of variations. Gavaskar believes Bumrah is overcomplicating his bowling but expects him to regain form soon.

title: Tamil Nadu election results: In Tirupattur, TVK man edges out minister by 1 vote
content: In a nail-biting finish, TVK candidate R Seenivasa Sethupathy narrowly defeated DMK minister K R Periyakaruppan by a single vote in the Tirupattur constituency. The final tally saw Sethupathy secure 83,375 votes against Periyakaruppan's 83,374, following a prolonged counting process that included re-verification of postal ballots.

title: 'Denied TMC ticket for refusing to pay Rs 5 crore': Ex-cricketer's shocking claim
content: The 40-year-old, who is Bengal’s highest run-scorer in first-class cricket with 10,195 runs, served as minister of state for sports in the Mamata Banerjee-led government. The government completed its 15-year term before the BJP won the latest assembly elections on Monday.

title: Chandigarh: Power bank catches fire on IndiGo flight; 6 injured in emergency evacuation
content: Panic erupted on an IndiGo flight from Hyderabad to Chandigarh when a power bank ignited mid-cabin after landing. Six passengers sustained injuries during the subsequent evacuation via emergency slides. Airport officials confirmed no fatalities, but passengers alleged delays in medical aid and inadequate complaint handling by the airline.

title: Inside Roger and Mirka Federer’s $1.1 Billion Net Worth: Tennis’ First Billionaire
content: Roger Federer and Mirka Federer’s combined net worth in 2026 is around $1.1 billion or more, based on Forbes. Most of this wealth comes from brand deals, business moves, and Federer’s On investment. Mirka played a key role in managing his career. Their success shows how smart planning beyond sports can build long-term financial strength.

title: 'Anga, Banga, Kalinga': BJP completes eastern arc with Bengal victory
content: The BJP has achieved a significant milestone by securing a victory in West Bengal in 2026, completing its symbolic arc across eastern India with control in Bihar, Bengal, and Odisha. This win consolidates a long-standing political project, marking a sharp turnaround for the party after a Lok Sabha setback and expanding its national reach.

title: Meet James Harrison: The man who donated blood for over 60 years and saved 2.4 million babies
content: James Harrison, dubbed the 'man with the golden arm,' donated plasma over 1,100 times, saving an estimated 2.4 million Australian babies. His rare antibodies produced a vital treatment preventing Rhesus disease, a dangerous pregnancy complication. Harrison's lifelong commitment, starting at 18, ensured generations of healthy births, transforming a once deadly condition.

title: Met Gala 2026: Fans roast Joe Burrow’s “safe” outfit choice
content: Cincinnati Bengals QB Joe Burrow's 2026 Met Gala appearance has sparked debate. While he arrived with teammate Justin Jefferson in a navy suit, fans found it underwhelming compared to previous years' bold fashion choices. Despite some defending his polished look, many expected more from the quarterback, especially after his 2024 backless suit moment.

title: CSK's disciplined bowling and Samson’s unbeaten 87 brush aside DC
content: Delhi Capitals’s home struggles continued as they managed only 155/7 against Chennai Super Kings, who chased the target comfortably thanks to Sanju Samson’s unbeaten 87. CSK’s disciplined bowling, led by Akeal Hosein, exposed DC’s batting issues again. Poor performances from KL Rahul, Axar Patel and Kuldeep Yadav pushed Delhi closer to elimination.

title: SIR, yes SIR: How voter deletions impacted West Bengal elections
content: West Bengal witnessed a significant political shift as the BJP formed its first government, largely influenced by the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. This exercise, which removed nearly 12% of voters, disproportionately affected minority communities and became a central, contentious issue, reshaping voting trends and contributing to the BJP's surge across the state.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for May 6
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 6, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: On Pakistan's Request, Trump Briefly Pauses Project Freedom, Blockade To Continue
content: Trump said he was pausing the 'Project Freedom' for a short period to give space for US efforts to finalise a settlement with Iran.

title: Banks On Edge, Finance Ministry On Alert: Mythos AI Panic Explained
content: Mythos, an advanced AI model developed by Anthropic, can compress weeks of hacking effort into hours. This is making banks nervous globally.

title: Trump, Xi Jinping Likely To Discuss Taiwan Next Week
content: Taiwan is likely to be a topic of conversation between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping when they meet next week, said Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

title: Jabalpur Boat Tragedy: Court Orders FIR Against Cruise Operator
content: The Jabalpur District Court has directed the registration of an FIR against the cruise boat pilot and others after the boat sank into a river in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh and claimed the lives of 13 individuals.

title: Blast Near Cantonment Area In Amritsar, No Casualties Reported: Cops
content: A blast occured in Khasa near the cantonment area in Amritsar at around 10:50 am on Tuesday, prompting a swift response from police and security agencies. No casualties were reported.

title: Trump Says Pausing US Operation To Help Ships Leave Hormuz
content: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he was halting the US military operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz after just one day, in a bid to reach a deal with Iran to end the Middle East war.

title: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Trump Pauses "Project Freedom" In Hormuz For Short Period
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

title: OpenAI President Claims Elon Musk Physically Threatened Him
content: According to Musk, the company pivoted to a for-profit structure and misappropriated his $38 million founding donation to build a company now valued at more than $850 billion.

title: Marco Rubio Says Offensive Action Against Iran Is Over
content: The United States has completed its offensive operations against Iran, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday, even as Washington warned it was ready to unleash a "devastating" response to any new attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Protesters Ask Arunachal Chief Minister To Step Aside Amid Corruption Probe
content: Arunachal Pradesh Chief Minister Pema Khandu has dismissed the corruption charges against him after the Supreme Court ordered the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to look into the matter.

title: India, US Very Close To Trade Deal, Says Trump Official
content: India and the US are "very, very close" to signing the trade deal and need to get over "that last hurdle", US Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau said on Tuesday.

title: 2 Killed, 3 Injured In Shooting In US' Dallas: Cops
content: A man shot five people in Texas on Tuesday, killing two, at a shopping mall north of Dallas, police said.

title: 'This Election Has Sent Tremors Through Entire Political Landscape': Vijay
content: Making its electoral debut, the actor-turned-politician's party shocked the DMK and AIADMK and secured 108 of 234 seats.

title: Centre Announces Rs 18,000-Crore Scheme For Airlines, MSMEs Hit By Iran War
content: Information and Broadcasting Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said the ECLGS 5.0, having an outlay of Rs 18,100 crore, would help airlines as well as MSMEs impacted by the West Asia crisis.

title: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Highlights: Vijay To Leave Trichy East, MK Stalin's Resignation Accepted
content: Tamil Nadu Assembly Election Results 2026 LIVE Updates: While most exit polls have predicted DMK's second straight win, one suggested a huge wave of the "Vijay effect" in the state this time.

title: "Backstabbers": Ally DMK On Congress' Possible Tie-Up With Actor Vijay
content: The five seats the Congress got in this election was because the party aligned with the DMK. "Otherwise, they would have drawn a blank at the recent polls," he said.

title: Explosions near BSF headquarters in Jalandhar and Army Cantonment in Amritsar cause panic
content: Following the explosion, a team of forensic and bomb disposal squads visited the site to collect samples and the investigation is underway

title: Stalin accorded rousing reception in Kolathur
content: The DMK president met and thanked supporters. Voters recalled his contributions to the constituency, including the construction of flyovers, community halls, and other facilities

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Making a strong statement with record margin
content: The IUML leader secured a massive victory margin of 85,327 votes over Left Democratic Front (LDF) candidate K.T. Mujeeb Rahman in Malappuram, setting a new benchmark in Kerala’s electoral history

title: BJP gears up to pick Bengal CM; Suvendu Adhikari front runner
content: Swearing-in of the new Cabinet is likely to take place on May 9, the birth anniversary of Rabindranath Tagore

title: Omar, Mehbooba question ECI’s role in West Bengal polls
content: The role of the EC played a significant part in the results besides other factors, says NC leader; PDP chief alleges “electoral manipulation” in West Bengal

title: Assembly election 2026 results: Congress mulls helping Vijay form ‘secular’ government in Tamil Nadu after he reaches out for support
content: Assam CM Himanta likely to submit resignation to Governor today; Congress CM race intensifies in Kerala

title: Will strengthen INDIA bloc, says Mamata after Bengal election debacle
content: For the first time, Mamata Banerjee has spoken about strengthening the Opposition alliance; the statement is significant as it comes after her defeat in the West Bengal Assembly election

title: 2026 Assam Assembly elections at a glance
content: The BJP won 82 of 126 seats, asserting their dominance in the State’s political landscape. Altogether, the BJP-led NEDA has swept the elections by winning 102 seats and the Congress-led Asom Sonmilito Morcha has secured 21 seats.

title: Assembly poll 2026: CPI(M) commences post-poll debacle stock-taking exercise as it realises there is no magic bullet
content: The debilitating rout, especially in the party’s strongholds, has thrown up some big questions regarding the party’s campaign strategy, organisational cohesiveness, candidate finalisation, and efficacy of its political operations, including whether “misplaced confidence” of an “inevitable” third consecutive term in power had imbued the party’s electioneering with complacency from the booth level and upwards

title: Madhya Pradesh court orders registration of FIR in Jabalpur boat tragedy that killed 13 people
content: Judicial Magistrate First Class D.P. Sutrakar said the “failure” of the cruise boat driver and other crew members to make any effort to rescue the drowning passengers constitutes an attempt to commit culpable homicide

title: Assembly election 2026 results: Tamil Nadu Congress offers conditional support to Vijay's TVK
content: The Tamil Nadu Congress Committee extended its support to the TVK, but said that the TVK shouldn't seek the support of the lone winner from the BJP in the State, or any alliance partners of the BJP

title: Odisha Chief Electoral Officer to meet BJD delegation over Rajya Sabha elections irregularity allegation
content: In a complaint, the BJD termed issuance of second ballot to two BJP MLAs during Rajya Sabha election as illegal

title: Hundeds of tribals, farmers march in Dahanu to submit land-related claims to Palghar district office
content: 15,000 peasants from the two tehsils of Dahanu and Talasari in Palghar district were present for the march, the CPI(M) said

title: Fraud unearthed in Rajasthan health scheme, doctor, lab operator arrested
content: The accused doctor allegedly prescribed unnecessary diagnostic tests without examining patients or even in their absence and based on these prescriptions, the lab operator generated fake test reports and uploaded them to the RGHS portal to claim payments

title: Govandi residents raise complaints with BMC for non-commencement of pre-monsoon desilting work
content: Lawyer Abdi Abas Sayyed, a Govandi resident, highlighted the non-commencement of pre-monsoon desilting work and submitted that the BMC has not started desilting work in wards 134, 136, 137, 138, 139, 140 and 141

title: Maharashtra to upgrade 19 secondary ashram schools to junior college for tribals
content: The decision was aimed at preventing dropouts and continuing the educational progress of tribal students in the hilly and remote areas of Maharashtra

title: Congress offers support to TVK but with a condition
content: TNCC says TVK, which emerged as the single-largest party, should not seek support from BJP or its alliance partners; Vijay likely to meet the Governor on May 6 to stake claim to form govt or seek time

title: Bihar Cabinet expansion on May 7, Modi, Shah and Nabin to attend event
content: Sources said most Ministers from the Nitish Kumar-led NDA government are likely to be retained, and a few fresh faces will be introduced to fill all the 36 Cabinet berths

title: How ‘bulldozer justice’ undermines the law
content: The image of swift destruction creates an impression of decisive leadership, but it also normalises the idea that executive authority can override legal safeguards

title: Gen Z entrepreneurs adept at speed, should focus on deep learning: speakers
content: Panellists at the discussion say if given reasonably good purpose, Gen Z entrepreneurs can spend time to do it

title: Cabinet panel clears Railway Ministry’s three multitracking projects in six States
content: The proposed capacity enhancement will improve rail connectivity to several prominent tourist destinations across the country

title: Bombay HC directs Maharashtra to ease travel curbs on U.K.-based doctor booked for posts on PM Modi
content: Sangram Patil, U.K.-based doctor and YouTuber and a British national of Indian origin, was stopped from returning to the United Kingdom after immigration authorities cited the LOC

title: U.P. man jailed over ‘I love Muhammad’ post secures bail
content: The court noted that the applicant ‘does not have any criminal antecedents’ and the post ‘does not name any particular caste or community’

title: Draft strict rules to deny parole to persons convicted of sexual offences, Maharashtra CM tells officials
content: The directive comes amid the case of a 65-year-old man with a criminal record who allegedly sexually assaulted and murdered a four-year-old girl at Narsapur in Bhor tehsil of Pune district on May 1

title: Karnataka HC upholds ‘leave India’ notice to French national who was operating restaurant while on tourist visa
content: French national Christophe Stephane Monxion had been regularly going back to his country for short periods and returning to India on a new tourist visa each time while operating a restaurant in Gokarna

title: M.K. Stalin resigns as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister; Governor accepts his resignation
content: “The Governor has requested him [Mr. Stalin] to continue in office until alternate arrangements are made,” a statement from the Lok Bhavan said.

title: Cabinet clears amendment to make insult to Vande Mataram a punishable offence
content: On February 6, the Union Ministry of Home Affairs in a set of instructions to States and other government bodies said that all the six stanzas of Vande Mataram, that comes to a little over three minutes long, should be sung or played at official events

title: Tiruchi orthography researchers decipher ancient inscription in Courtallam hill cave
content: After two-and-a-half years of research, K. Balakrishnan and Mathur B. Pavendan have deciphered an inscription in Sanyasi Pudavu in Courtallam hills in Tenkasi district

title: Dutch warship De Ruyter visits Kochi port
content: The ship’s crew members to engage in joint training exercises with the Indian Navy to strengthen operational ties

title: The highest and lowest margins of victory in the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly election
content: Most constituencies with margins above 60,000 votes were won by the TVK, with the DMK finishing second in a majority of these seats. The distribution of these margins shows a concentration in urban and industrial regions

title: Congress slams the government over ‘sluggish’ private investment, cites Chief Economic Adviser’s comment
content: Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh said that for some time now, his party has been drawing attention to a fundamental problem preventing the economy from achieving higher real GDP growth rates

title: Union Cabinet raises sugarcane FRP by ₹10 per quintal
content: The government said that with a view to protect the interests of sugarcane farmers, it has also decided that there shall not be any deduction in case of sugar mills where recovery is below 9.5%

title: Mann meets Murmu, seeks nixing of Rajya Sabha membership of seven MPs who quit AAP
content: His visit came after Raghav Chadha, one of the seven MPs who defected, met the President and submitted a representation alleging that the ruling party in Punjab is resorting to ‘vendetta politics’

title: India’s energy security amid conflicts
content: The conflict in West Asia has shown how geopolitical shocks can transmit to India’s domestic economy, given that the country imports over 85% of its crude needs. In a fragmented energy market, India’s edge lies not in self-sufficiency but in optionality.

title: Child among two missing after boat capsizes in Rajasthan
content: Preliminary investigation suggested that overloading might have caused the accident; the boat was being operated by a local and the occupants had no life jackets

title: Manipur’s Zomi leaders to take late BJP MLA Valte’s body to Delhi, protest outside party office
content: Valte was assaulted by an Imphal mob on May 4, 2023, suffering life-threatening injuries; after his death in February 2026, his family refused to perform his last rites till the arrest of those behind the attack

title: Put petty politics aside, says Rahul Gandhi, as he backs Mamata Banerjee
content: The Congress leader describes the BJP’s victory in West Bengal and Assam Assembly elections as a “theft” of the popular mandate

content: motherta banerjee refuses to resign as west Bengal chief minister . she alleged a 'large-scale manipulation' of the electoral process . her party won 80 seats in the 294-member assembly, while BJP's tally was 207 .

content: the scripture provides moral and spiritual guidance for everyday life in 212 Sanskrit verses . the manuscript is one of the oldest known copies written by the author himself . it will next be on loan at Shree Swaminarayan Temple, Stanmore, from may 13 .

content: Rajiv G is a senior assistant editor with the Times of India . he began his journey in 1998 with the Indian Express in Coimbatore before moving on to Deccan Chronicle in Hyderabad . since joining the Times in 2011, he has been at the forefront of reporting on Kerala’s health and political landscape .

title: Original Sabarimala PIL should’ve been binned: Supreme Court
content: the bench led by CJI Surya Kant said the SC in 2006 should have outrightly “thrown in the dustbin’ the original PIL that led to quashing of Sabarimala Ayyappa Temple custom restricting entry of women in the 10 to 50 age group . the bench said SC at best could have ordered investigations into the misconduct to bring the guilty to the book, but based on newspaper reports, the PIL could not have been entertained .

title: West Bengal: New CM likely to take oath on May 9; Adhikari frontrunner
content: the outgoing assembly set to be dissolved anytime and newly elected BJP MLAs likely to endorse party’s CM pick before may 9 . sources said the party was also considering appointment of a deputy CM . the governor may soon announce dissolution of the state assembly, which runs out on Friday .

title: Cabinet clears proposal to equate Vande Mataram with Jana Gana Mana
content: cabinet approves proposal to amend the Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act . it would put national song Vande Mataram on the same footing as the national Anthem, Jana Gana Mana . the law provides jail, fine or both for insults to national flag and Constitution .

title: Power shift: NDA tightens grip on big states, opposition shrinks
content: the governing alliance is now in office in five of them that have a combined LS strength of 229 . of six heavyweight states with 278 Lok Sabha seats, a total of over half of parliament's 543 seats . BJP-led NDA has never been so feeble in battleground states which hold the key to parliamentary majority .

title: ‘Historic, decisive win’: Trump congratulates PM Modi over Bengal results
content:
PM Modi and Donald Trump

US President Donald Trump has congratulated PM Modi on his “historic” election victory, a White House spokesman said Tuesday, in an apparent take on the recent round of assembly polls that were headlined by BJP’s big win in West Bengal. White House spokesman Kush Desai spoke of Trump’s warm sentiments for Modi, noting that the US president in their phone call last month expressed his admiration for Modi and “how lucky India is to have him as its leader”. “The President congratulates PM Modi on this recent, historic, and decisive election victory,” Desai said. On Monday, BJP ended the 15-year stint of Mamata Banerjee.

End of Article
